title: User testing + prompt iteration
steps: Share URL with 2-3 software engineers. Ask them to build a presentation for their work.
Analyze feedback: friction points, missing questions, quality of output.
Iterate on prompt. Evaluate Haiku 4.5 quality. Document findings.
acceptance_criteria: At least one prompt iteration based on feedback

positive: Asked useful concrete questions: 'who is your audience?' and 'give me a concrete example of [CLAIM]'
Great at focusing on the important things
negative: Feels like an interrogation, not collaborative — too many questions, repetitive
Too insistent on finding a 'problem to solve' framing — not every presentation fits that
No markdown formatting in responses — walls of plain text
Users want clickable suggestion buttons instead of typing every response
Takes too long to develop a presentation — needs to be punchier
requested_features: Quick-reply suggestion stubs/buttons above chat input

outcome.prompt_iteration: Folded into TASK-008 — collaborative tone rewrite, less repetitive questioning, loosen rigid problem framing, suggestion-driven interaction
